texto: Jack shook his head. 'Books. What is it with women and books? My sisters were the same. They were always buying books for boys they fancied.'Ellie bent down and picked up the stone and put it on the table. 'It's like sending a love letter without having to write it yourself,' she said softly.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Hazel Osmond: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “Jack shook his head. 'Books. What is it with women and books? My sisters were the same. They were…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amor, books, courtship pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e courtship.
